title: Search is really slow
body: The search feature used to be fast but now it takes forever. Sometimes it takes 10-15 seconds to get results. I have a lot of tasks but this seems excessive. Running TaskFlow on my work laptop.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this. A few things would help me narrow this down quickly:

When you say search 'used to be fast,' can you pinpoint roughly when it became slow? Did it coincide with anything — a TaskFlow update, a large import of tasks, or a change in your setup?

Also, approximately how many tasks are we talking about — hundreds, thousands, tens of thousands?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I think it started being slow maybe 2 weeks ago? I'm pretty sure I updated TaskFlow around that time — went from 2.2 to 2.3 if I remember right. I have around 5,000 tasks, I've been using it for a couple years now. I didn't do any big import or anything like that, just the normal update.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for those details — a v2.2 → v2.3 update with 5,000 tasks is a really useful starting point.

To dig deeper: is *every* search slow, or only certain kinds? For example, does searching for a single common word take just as long as searching for something very specific? And is it consistently 10-15 seconds, or does it vary — say, faster on a second search right after the first?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Now that you mention it, I think searching by task title is still pretty fast actually. It's when I search across the full descriptions that it gets really slow. Some of my tasks have pretty long descriptions — I paste meeting notes into them sometimes. The slowness seems pretty consistent, like 10-15 seconds every time I do a description search. I haven't noticed it getting faster on repeated searches.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's very helpful — the pattern of fast title search but slow description search after the v2.3 update is a strong signal.

Here's my working theory: the v2.3 update likely changed how full-text search works on task descriptions — possibly dropped an index or switched to a different search method that doesn't scale well with large description fields like yours.

To test this: could you try creating a quick test? Search descriptions for something that only appears in one or two tasks versus something common that appears in many. If both take the same ~10-15 seconds, that confirms the bottleneck is scanning *all* descriptions rather than matching — which would point squarely at a missing index or unoptimized query in v2.3.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Hmm okay, I just tried that. I searched for a pretty unique project name that I know is only in like 2 task descriptions, and then I searched for 'meeting' which is probably in hundreds of them. Both took about the same amount of time — maybe 12-13 seconds each. So yeah, sounds like it's scanning everything regardless. I also noticed my laptop fan kicks on when I do the search, if that helps at all.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Full-text description search performs full table scan after v2.3 upgrade (~12s for 5,000 tasks)","problem":"Since upgrading from TaskFlow v2.2 to v2.3, searching across task descriptions takes 10-15 seconds consistently. Title-only search remains fast. The user has approximately 5,000 tasks, many with long descriptions containing pasted meeting notes. The slowdown is CPU-bound (laptop fan spins up) and shows no caching improvement on repeated queries.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The v2.3 update likely removed, broke, or replaced the full-text index on the task descriptions column — or switched the description search implementation from an indexed full-text query to a naive unindexed scan (e.g., LIKE '%term%' or in-memory regex). Evidence: (1) title search is still fast (its index was preserved), (2) search time is constant regardless of how many results match (points to scanning all rows, not result processing), (3) CPU spike indicates row-by-row computation rather than index lookup, (4) no caching benefit rules out a warm-cache optimization path.","reproduction_steps":["Install TaskFlow v2.3 (or upgrade from v2.2 to v2.3)","Have a dataset with ~5,000 tasks, some with long description fields","Perform a description search for any term (common or rare)","Observe ~10-15 second response time and high CPU utilization","Compare with a title-only search on the same dataset — title search returns quickly"],"environment":"TaskFlow v2.3 (upgraded from v2.2), ~5,000 tasks with some containing long descriptions (pasted meeting notes), running on a work laptop (specific OS and DB engine not confirmed)","severity":"medium","impact":"Any user with a non-trivial number of tasks (~thousands) who searches by description will experience 10-15 second delays. Title-only search still works as a partial workaround. The feature is usable but painfully slow, degrading daily workflow for power users.","recommended_fix":"1. Diff the search query/implementation between v2.2 and v2.3 — look for changes to how description search is executed (check for dropped indexes, removed FTS configuration, or switch from indexed to LIKE/regex scan). 2. Verify the full-text index on the descriptions column exists and is being used (run EXPLAIN/query plan). 3. If the index was dropped, restore it. If the implementation changed, revert the description search path to the indexed approach. 4. Test with a dataset of 5,000+ tasks with large description fields to confirm sub-second response times are restored.","proposed_test_case":"Create a test dataset with 5,000 tasks where at least 100 have descriptions >500 characters. Run a description search for a term appearing in only 2 tasks and a term appearing in 500 tasks. Assert both return in under 2 seconds. Run the same test with title search to confirm parity. Verify via query plan that the description search uses an index, not a sequential scan.","information_gaps":["Exact database backend (SQLite, PostgreSQL, etc.) — though the fix direction is the same regardless","Whether v2.3 release notes or changelog mention search changes","Exact OS and hardware specs of the reporter's laptop"]}
